content.text:
Project Overview & Architecture

  The Collegiate Athletics & Learning Ops API Platform unifies league
  scheduling, instructional content governance, and compliance
  traceability for a department running seasons and training programs on
   a single offline server. This is a backend-only API with no frontend.

  User Roles:
  - Administrator – configures master data, security, and system
  settings
  - Scheduler – manages seasons, match scheduling, referee/staff
  assignments
  - Instructor – manages courses, chapters/units, teaching resources
  - Reviewer – participates in configurable multi-level approval
  workflows
  - Finance Clerk – records payments, signs postings, manages
  reconciliation
  - Auditor – read-only access to audit logs and compliance data

  Tech Stack:
  - Backend: Go with Echo framework
  - Database Access: sqlx
  - Database: PostgreSQL
  - Deployment: Single Docker container, no external network
  dependencies
  - Auth: JWT-based (30-minute access tokens, rotating server-side
  refresh tokens)
  - Password Hashing: bcrypt
  - File Storage: Local disk with SHA-256 fingerprints

  Key Constraints:
  - Fully offline – no external network dependencies
  - No frontend – API only
  - p95 < 300ms for typical queries under 200 concurrent sessions
  - Structured logs and local health/metrics endpoints for offline
  observability
  - Retention tiers: access logs 30 days, operation logs 180 days, audit
   logs 7 years
  - Tamper-evident hashing chains per day for audit logs

  API Categories: Authentication, Accounts, Courses, Resources, Matches,
   Reviews, Moderation, Payments (offline ledger), Audits. Each supports
   create/query/update/state-transition operations with strict role
  boundaries.

  This prompt establishes the foundation. No code generation required.
I've read the project overview. This is a Go/Echo backend API platform for collegiate athletics and learning operations. What would you like to work on?

Authentication, Accounts & Security

  Build Authentication and Account APIs in Go (Echo + sqlx +
  PostgreSQL).

  Authentication APIs: Local username/password only. JWT-based with
  short-lived access tokens (30 minutes) and rotating refresh tokens
  stored server-side. No external auth providers.

  Password Policy: bcrypt hashing, minimum 12 characters with complexity
   requirements (at least 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 number). Login
  lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts.

  Accounts Table: unique username, password_hash, role enum
  (Administrator, Scheduler, Instructor, Reviewer, Finance Clerk,
  Auditor), status enum (Active, Frozen, Deactivated).

  Device Fingerprinting: Optional device fingerprinting using salted
  local hashes of user agent + stable device attributes. Devices table
  with fingerprint_hash unique per account.

  Security Controls: Privilege-escalation prevention by server-side role
   checks only. Rate limiting per account. Sensitive-field masking in
  responses (e.g., only last 2 characters of student/employee IDs
  visible). All endpoints enforce strict role boundaries.

  Single Docker deployment, no external dependencies.

text.text:
Good, I have all the information needed. Here is the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Authentication, Accounts & Security System

### Project Overview

A greenfield Go backend API providing authentication, account management, device fingerprinting, and security controls. Single Docker deployment with PostgreSQL, using Echo v4, sqlx, JWT (golang-jwt/jwt/v5), and bcrypt.

---

### 1. File Structure

```
repo/
├── cmd/
│   └── server/
│       └── main.go                  # Entry point: config load, DB connect, wire dependencies, start Echo
├── internal/
│   ├── config/
│   │   └── config.go               # Env-based configuration struct + loader
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── account.go              # Account struct, Role/Status enums
│   │   ├── device.go               # Device (fingerprint) struct
│   │   ├── refresh_token.go        # RefreshToken struct
│   │   └── login_attempt.go        # LoginAttempt struct (for lockout tracking)
│   ├── repository/
│   │   ├── account_repo.go         # CRUD for accounts table
│   │   ├── device_repo.go          # CRUD for devices table
│   │   ├── refresh_token_repo.go   # CRUD for refresh_tokens table
│   │   └── login_attempt_repo.go   # Insert/query for login_attempts table
│   ├── service/
│   │   ├── auth_service.go         # Login, logout, refresh, password validation, lockout logic
│   │   ├── account_service.go      # Account CRUD, status transitions, role assignment
│   │   ├── token_service.go        # JWT creation/validation, refresh token rotation
│   │   └── device_service.go       # Fingerprint hashing, device registration/lookup
│   ├── handler/
│   │   ├── auth_handler.go         # POST /auth/login, POST /auth/refresh, POST /auth/logout
│   │   └── account_handler.go      # GET/POST/PUT /accounts, GET /accounts/:id
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   ├── jwt_auth.go             # JWT extraction + validation middleware
│   │   ├── role_guard.go           # Role-based access control middleware
│   │   └── rate_limiter.go         # Per-account rate limiting middleware
│   ├── dto/
│   │   ├── auth_dto.go             # LoginRequest, LoginResponse, RefreshRequest, RefreshResponse
│   │   └── account_dto.go          # CreateAccountRequest, UpdateAccountRequest, AccountResponse (with masking)
│   └── router/
│       └── router.go               # Route registration, group definitions, middleware wiring
├── migrations/
│   ├── 001_create_accounts.up.sql
│   ├── 001_create_accounts.down.sql
│   ├── 002_create_refresh_tokens.up.sql
│   ├── 002_create_refresh_tokens.down.sql
│   ├── 003_create_devices.up.sql
│   ├── 003_create_devices.down.sql
│   ├── 004_create_login_attempts.up.sql
│   └── 004_create_login_attempts.down.sql
├── go.mod
├── go.sum
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
└── .env.example
```

---

### 2. `go.mod` Setup

Module name: `github.com/eaglepoint/authapi` (or similar). Go version: `go 1.22` (minimum for all dependencies).

**Required dependencies:**

| Module | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `github.com/labstack/echo/v4` | HTTP framework |
| `github.com/jmoiron/sqlx` | SQL extensions |
| `github.com/lib/pq` | PostgreSQL driver |
| `github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5` | JWT tokens |
| `golang.org/x/crypto` | bcrypt (via `golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt`) |
| `golang.org/x/time` | Rate limiting (via `golang.org/x/time/rate`) |
| `github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4` | Database migrations |
| `github.com/google/uuid` | UUIDs for tokens/IDs |

---

### 3. Database Schema (Migrations)

#### `migrations/001_create_accounts.up.sql`

```sql
CREATE TYPE account_role AS ENUM (
    'Administrator',
    'Scheduler',
    'Instructor',
    'Reviewer',
    'Finance Clerk',
    'Auditor'
);

CREATE TYPE account_status AS ENUM (
    'Active',
    'Frozen',
    'Deactivated'
);

CREATE TABLE accounts (
    id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    username      VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    role          account_role NOT NULL,
    status        account_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Active',
    created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_accounts_username ON accounts (username);
CREATE INDEX idx_accounts_status ON accounts (status);
```

#### `migrations/001_create_accounts.down.sql`

```sql
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS accounts;
DROP TYPE IF EXISTS account_status;
DROP TYPE IF EXISTS account_role;
```

#### `migrations/002_create_refresh_tokens.up.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE refresh_tokens (
    id           UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    account_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES accounts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    token_hash   VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    device_id    UUID,          -- nullable; links to devices table if fingerprint provided
    expires_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    revoked      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_refresh_tokens_account ON refresh_tokens (account_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_refresh_tokens_hash ON refresh_tokens (token_hash);
```

Key design decision: Store only a SHA-256 hash of the refresh token, never the raw value. The raw token is returned to the client once. On rotation, the old row is marked `revoked = TRUE` and a new row is inserted. This enables **refresh token reuse detection**: if a revoked token is ever presented, all tokens for that account are revoked (potential theft).

#### `migrations/002_create_refresh_tokens.down.sql`

```sql
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS refresh_tokens;
```

#### `migrations/003_create_devices.up.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE devices (
    id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    account_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES accounts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    fingerprint_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    device_name      VARCHAR(255),      -- optional human-readable label
    last_seen_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE (account_id, fingerprint_hash)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_devices_account ON devices (account_id);
```

The `fingerprint_hash` is computed server-side: `SHA-256(salt + user_agent + stable_device_attributes)`. The salt is per-application (from config), not per-row, so the same device+account always produces the same hash for lookup.

#### `migrations/003_create_devices.down.sql`

```sql
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS devices;
```

#### `migrations/004_create_login_attempts.up.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE login_attempts (
    id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    account_id  UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES accounts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    success     BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
    ip_address  INET,
    attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_login_attempts_account_time ON login_attempts (account_id, attempted_at DESC);
```

Lockout logic queries: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM login_attempts WHERE account_id = $1 AND success = FALSE AND attempted_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes'`. If count >= 5, reject login.

#### `migrations/004_create_login_attempts.down.sql`

```sql
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS login_attempts;
```

---

### 4. Internal Package Details

#### 4a. `internal/config/config.go`

A single `Config` struct populated from environment variables (with sane defaults for local dev). Fields:

```
ServerPort           string   // default ":8080"
DatabaseURL          string   // "postgres://user:pass@db:5432/authdb?sslmode=disable"
JWTSecret            string   // HMAC-SHA256 signing key (min 32 bytes)
AccessTokenTTL       time.Duration  // 30m
RefreshTokenTTL      time.Duration  // 7d (configurable)
DeviceFingerprintSalt string  // static salt for fingerprint hashing
BcryptCost           int      // 12 (default)
MaxLoginAttempts     int      // 5
LockoutDuration      time.Duration  // 15m
RateLimitRPS         float64  // requests per second per account
RateLimitBurst       int      // burst size
```

Load via `os.Getenv` with fallbacks. No external config library needed for this scope.

#### 4b. `internal/models/`

**`account.go`**: `Account` struct with `db` tags for sqlx. Custom types `Role` and `Status` as `string` types with constants and a `Scan`/`Value` implementation for database/sql driver compatibility with PostgreSQL enums.

```go
type Role string
const (
    RoleAdministrator  Role = "Administrator"
    RoleScheduler      Role = "Scheduler"
    RoleInstructor     Role = "Instructor"
    RoleReviewer       Role = "Reviewer"
    RoleFinanceClerk   Role = "Finance Clerk"
    RoleAuditor        Role = "Auditor"
)

type Status string
const (
    StatusActive      Status = "Active"
    StatusFrozen      Status = "Frozen"
    StatusDeactivated Status = "Deactivated"
)

type Account struct {
    ID           uuid.UUID  `db:"id" json:"id"`
    Username     string     `db:"username" json:"username"`
    PasswordHash string     `db:"password_hash" json:"-"`  // never serialized
    Role         Role       `db:"role" json:"role"`
    Status       Status     `db:"status" json:"status"`
    CreatedAt    time.Time  `db:"created_at" json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt    time.Time  `db:"updated_at" json:"updated_at"`
}
```

**`refresh_token.go`**: `RefreshToken` struct with `ID`, `AccountID`, `TokenHash`, `DeviceID` (nullable `*uuid.UUID`), `ExpiresAt`, `Revoked`, `CreatedAt`.

**`device.go`**: `Device` struct with `ID`, `AccountID`, `FingerprintHash`, `DeviceName` (nullable), `LastSeenAt`, `CreatedAt`.

**`login_attempt.go`**: `LoginAttempt` struct with `ID`, `AccountID`, `Success`, `IPAddress`, `AttemptedAt`.

#### 4c. `internal/repository/`

Each repo file takes a `*sqlx.DB` (or `sqlx.ExtContext` for testability) and provides methods:

**`account_repo.go`**:
- `Create(ctx, account) error`
- `GetByID(ctx, id) (*Account, error)`
- `GetByUsername(ctx, username) (*Account, error)`
- `UpdateStatus(ctx, id, status) error`
- `UpdatePassword(ctx, id, passwordHash) error`
- `List(ctx, offset, limit) ([]Account, error)`

**`refresh_token_repo.go`**:
- `Create(ctx, token) error`
- `GetByHash(ctx, tokenHash) (*RefreshToken, error)`
- `Revoke(ctx, id) error`
- `RevokeAllForAccount(ctx, accountID) error` -- used on theft detection or logout-all

**`device_repo.go`**:
- `Upsert(ctx, device) (*Device, error)` -- INSERT ON CONFLICT (account_id, fingerprint_hash) DO UPDATE last_seen_at
- `ListByAccount(ctx, accountID) ([]Device, error)`
- `Delete(ctx, id) error`

**`login_attempt_repo.go`**:
- `Record(ctx, attempt) error`
- `CountRecentFailures(ctx, accountID, window time.Duration) (int, error)`

#### 4d. `internal/service/`

**`token_service.go`** -- JWT creation and validation:
- `GenerateAccessToken(account) (string, error)` -- creates a signed JWT with claims: `sub` (account ID), `username`, `role`, `exp` (now + 30min), `iat`. Uses `jwt.SigningMethodHS256`.
- `ValidateAccessToken(tokenString) (*Claims, error)` -- parses and validates, returns custom `Claims` struct.
- `GenerateRefreshToken() (rawToken string, hashedToken string, error)` -- generates a cryptographically random 32-byte token via `crypto/rand`, returns both the raw (hex-encoded, sent to client) and SHA-256 hash (stored in DB).
- `HashRefreshToken(raw string) string` -- deterministic SHA-256 for lookup.

**`auth_service.go`** -- orchestration layer:
- `Login(ctx, username, password, deviceFingerprint, ipAddress) (*LoginResponse, error)`:
  1. Look up account by username. If not found, return generic "invalid credentials" (no user enumeration).
  2. Check account status. If Frozen or Deactivated, reject with appropriate message.
  3. Query `CountRecentFailures`. If >= 5, return "account locked" with remaining lockout time.
  4. Compare password with bcrypt. If mismatch, record failed attempt, return "invalid credentials".
  5. Record successful attempt.
  6. If device fingerprint provided, upsert device record.
  7. Generate access token + refresh token. Store refresh token hash in DB (linked to device if applicable).
  8. Return tokens + account info.

- `Refresh(ctx, rawRefreshToken) (*LoginResponse, error)`:
  1. Hash the provided token, look up in DB.
  2. If not found, return error.
  3. If `revoked == TRUE` -- **reuse detection**: revoke ALL tokens for that account, return error. This forces re-login on all devices.
  4. If expired, return error.
  5. Revoke the current token.
  6. Generate new access + refresh token pair (rotation).
  7. Return new tokens.

- `Logout(ctx, rawRefreshToken) error`: Hash token, mark revoked.

- `ValidatePassword(password string) error`: Enforce policy (min 12 chars, 1 upper, 1 lower, 1 digit). Return descriptive error listing which requirements failed.

**`account_service.go`**:
- `CreateAccount(ctx, req) (*Account, error)`: Validate password policy, hash with bcrypt, insert. Only callable by Administrator.
- `GetAccount(ctx, id) (*Account, error)`: Retrieve by ID.
- `ListAccounts(ctx, offset, limit) ([]Account, error)`.
- `UpdateAccountStatus(ctx, id, newStatus) error`: Only Administrator can freeze/deactivate. When deactivating, also call `RevokeAllForAccount` to invalidate all sessions.
- `ChangePassword(ctx, accountID, oldPassword, newPassword) error`: Verify old password, validate new password policy, update hash, revoke all refresh tokens (force re-login on all devices).

**`device_service.go`**:
- `ComputeFingerprint(userAgent string, attributes map[string]string) string`: Concatenates salt + user_agent + sorted stable attributes, computes SHA-256, returns hex string. The attributes map could include screen resolution, timezone offset, language, etc. -- all sent by the client and hashed server-side.
- `RegisterOrUpdateDevice(ctx, accountID, fingerprintHash, deviceName) (*Device, error)`.
- `ListDevices(ctx, accountID) ([]Device, error)`.

#### 4e. `internal/dto/`

**`auth_dto.go`**:
```go
type LoginRequest struct {
    Username          string            `json:"username" validate:"required"`
    Password          string            `json:"password" validate:"required"`
    DeviceFingerprint *DeviceFingerprint `json:"device_fingerprint,omitempty"` // optional
}

type DeviceFingerprint struct {
    UserAgent  string            `json:"user_agent"`
    Attributes map[string]string `json:"attributes"` // screen_resolution, timezone, language, etc.
}

type LoginResponse struct {
    AccessToken  string `json:"access_token"`
    RefreshToken string `json:"refresh_token"`
    ExpiresIn    int    `json:"expires_in"` // seconds until access token expiry
}

type RefreshRequest struct {
    RefreshToken string `json:"refresh_token" validate:"required"`
}
```

**`account_dto.go`**:
```go
type CreateAccountRequest struct {
    Username string `json:"username" validate:"required,min=3,max=255"`
    Password string `json:"password" validate:"required"`
    Role     string `json:"role" validate:"required"`
}

type AccountResponse struct {
    ID        uuid.UUID `json:"id"`
    Username  string    `json:"username"`
    Role      string    `json:"role"`
    Status    string    `json:"status"`
    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
}
// Note: password_hash is NEVER included. This is the "masking" for sensitive fields.
// The Account model uses json:"-" on PasswordHash, and the DTO explicitly excludes it.
```

#### 4f. `internal/handler/`

**`auth_handler.go`**:
- `POST /auth/login` -- binds `LoginRequest`, calls `auth_service.Login`, returns 200 with tokens or 401/423 (locked).
- `POST /auth/refresh` -- binds `RefreshRequest`, calls `auth_service.Refresh`, returns 200 with new tokens or 401.
- `POST /auth/logout` -- requires JWT auth middleware, binds refresh token from body, calls `auth_service.Logout`, returns 204.

**`account_handler.go`**:
- `POST /accounts` -- requires Administrator role. Binds `CreateAccountRequest`, calls `account_service.CreateAccount`.
- `GET /accounts` -- requires Administrator role. Calls `account_service.ListAccounts` with pagination query params.
- `GET /accounts/:id` -- requires Administrator role OR self-access (account ID matches JWT sub). Returns `AccountResponse` (masked).
- `PUT /accounts/:id/status` -- requires Administrator role. Updates account status.
- `PUT /accounts/:id/password` -- requires self-access only. Changes password.

#### 4g. `internal/middleware/`

**`jwt_auth.go`**:
Extracts `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header, calls `token_service.ValidateAccessToken`, stores claims in Echo context (`c.Set("claims", claims)`). Returns 401 if invalid/expired.

**`role_guard.go`**:
Factory function `RequireRoles(roles ...Role) echo.MiddlewareFunc`. Reads claims from context, checks if the account's role is in the allowed list. Returns 403 if not authorized.

**`rate_limiter.go`**:
Uses `golang.org/x/time/rate`. Maintains a `sync.Map` keyed by account ID (extracted from JWT claims after auth middleware). Each entry is a `*rate.Limiter`. If no JWT present (public endpoints), key by IP address. Returns 429 if rate exceeded. Includes a background goroutine to evict stale entries every 10 minutes.

#### 4h. `internal/router/router.go`

Wires everything together:

```go
func Setup(e *echo.Echo, h *Handlers, mw *Middlewares) {
    // Public routes (no auth required, IP-based rate limiting)
    auth := e.Group("/auth")
    auth.POST("/login", h.Auth.Login)
    auth.POST("/refresh", h.Auth.Refresh)

    // Protected routes (JWT required)
    api := e.Group("/api", mw.JWTAuth, mw.RateLimiter)

    api.POST("/auth/logout", h.Auth.Logout)

    // Account management (Administrator only, except self-access)
    accounts := api.Group("/accounts", mw.RequireRoles(models.RoleAdministrator))
    accounts.POST("", h.Account.Create)
    accounts.GET("", h.Account.List)
    accounts.GET("/:id", h.Account.Get)        // handler internally also allows self-access
    accounts.PUT("/:id/status", h.Account.UpdateStatus)

    // Self-service (any authenticated user for their own account)
    api.PUT("/accounts/:id/password", h.Account.ChangePassword) // handler checks self-access
}
```

#### 4i. `cmd/server/main.go`

Responsibilities in order:
1. Load config from environment.
2. Connect to PostgreSQL using `sqlx.Connect("postgres", cfg.DatabaseURL)` with retry loop (wait for DB to be ready in Docker).
3. Run migrations using `golang-migrate/migrate/v4` with `source/file` and `database/postgres` drivers, pointing at `./migrations/`.
4. Instantiate all repositories (pass `*sqlx.DB`).
5. Instantiate all services (pass repositories + config).
6. Instantiate all handlers (pass services).
7. Create Echo instance, register global middleware (logger, recover, CORS).
8. Call `router.Setup(e, handlers, middlewares)`.
9. Start server on configured port.
10. Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM using `e.Shutdown(ctx)`.

---

### 5. Docker Configuration

#### `Dockerfile`

Multi-stage build:
- **Stage 1 (builder)**: `golang:1.22-alpine`. Copy `go.mod`, `go.sum`, run `go mod download`. Copy source, `CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -o /app/server ./cmd/server`.
- **Stage 2 (runtime)**: `alpine:3.19`. Copy binary from builder. Copy `migrations/` directory. Expose port 8080. `CMD ["/app/server"]`.

#### `docker-compose.yml`

```yaml
version: "3.9"
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: authuser
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: authpass
      POSTGRES_DB: authdb
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U authuser -d authdb"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  api:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    environment:
      SERVER_PORT: ":8080"
      DATABASE_URL: "postgres://authuser:authpass@db:5432/authdb?sslmode=disable"
      JWT_SECRET: "change-me-to-a-real-secret-at-least-32-bytes"
      DEVICE_FINGERPRINT_SALT: "change-me-device-salt"
      BCRYPT_COST: "12"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

This satisfies the "single Docker deployment, no external dependencies" requirement -- `docker-compose up` brings up both services with the API auto-migrating the database on startup.

#### `.env.example`

Documents all expected environment variables with placeholder values and explanatory comments.

---

### 6. Security Design Decisions

**Refresh token rotation with reuse detection**: When a refresh token is used, it is immediately revoked and a new one is issued. If a revoked token is ever presented again (indicating it was stolen and the legitimate user already rotated), ALL tokens for that account are revoked. This is the OWASP-recommended pattern.

**No user enumeration**: Login returns the same generic error ("invalid credentials") whether the username does not exist or the password is wrong. Timing is normalized by always running bcrypt even for non-existent users (compare against a dummy hash).

**Password hash never leaves the server**: The `Account` model uses `json:"-"` on `PasswordHash`. The `AccountResponse` DTO does not include it at all. Double protection.

**Rate limiting is per-account, not just per-IP**: This prevents a single compromised account from being used to hammer the API, regardless of IP rotation. Public endpoints (login, refresh) are rate-limited by IP.

**Lockout is time-based, not permanent**: 5 failed attempts in a 15-minute window triggers a 15-minute lockout. The `login_attempts` table provides an audit trail. No counter to reset -- the query simply counts failures in the rolling window.

**Device fingerprinting is optional**: The client may or may not send device attributes. If sent, the server computes a salted hash and associates it with the refresh token. This allows features like "active sessions" listing and "log out of unknown devices."

---

### 7. Implementation Sequence

1. **Phase 1 -- Skeleton**: `go.mod`, `main.go`, `config.go`, `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`. Verify the app starts and connects to Postgres.
2. **Phase 2 -- Database**: All four migration files. Run migrations on startup. Verify tables exist.
3. **Phase 3 -- Models + Repositories**: All model structs, all repository files. Unit-testable with a test database.
4. **Phase 4 -- Token Service**: JWT generation/validation, refresh token generation/hashing. Unit tests with known secrets.
5. **Phase 5 -- Auth Service + Handlers**: Login, refresh, logout. Integration test the full flow.
6. **Phase 6 -- Account Service + Handlers**: CRUD, status transitions, password change.
7. **Phase 7 -- Middleware**: JWT auth, role guard, rate limiter. Wire into router.
8. **Phase 8 -- Device Fingerprinting**: Device service, integration with login flow. Optional path -- login works without it.
9. **Phase 9 -- Polish**: Error handling consistency (custom error types mapped to HTTP codes), request validation (Echo's validator with `go-playground/validator`), structured logging, graceful shutdown.

---

### 8. Key Patterns and Conventions

- **Constructor injection**: Every service/handler receives its dependencies via constructor (`NewAuthService(repo, tokenSvc, cfg)`). No global state.
- **Context propagation**: All repository methods accept `context.Context` as first argument for cancellation and timeouts.
- **Error wrapping**: Use `fmt.Errorf("account_repo.GetByUsername: %w", err)` for stack traces. Services translate repo errors into domain errors; handlers translate domain errors into HTTP status codes.
- **No ORM**: Raw SQL with sqlx named queries. Explicit, auditable, no magic.
- **Migrations run automatically**: `main.go` runs `m.Up()` on startup. If already migrated, it is a no-op. For production, this could be gated by a config flag.

---

Build compiles cleanly. Here's what was built:

## Project Structure

**29 files** across a clean layered architecture:

### Database (4 migrations)
- `accounts` table — UUID PK, unique username, password_hash, role enum (6 roles), status enum (Active/Frozen/Deactivated)
- `refresh_tokens` table — SHA-256 hashed tokens, revocation flag, device linkage
- `devices` table — fingerprint_hash unique per account, last-seen tracking
- `login_attempts` table — success/fail tracking with IP, indexed by account+time

### API Endpoints
| Method | Path | Auth | Access |
|--------|------|------|--------|
| POST | `/auth/login` | Public | All |
| POST | `/auth/refresh` | Public | All |
| POST | `/api/auth/logout` | JWT | Any authenticated |
| POST | `/api/accounts` | JWT | Administrator only |
| GET | `/api/accounts` | JWT | Administrator only |
| GET | `/api/accounts/:id` | JWT | Administrator or self |
| PUT | `/api/accounts/:id/status` | JWT | Administrator only |
| PUT | `/api/accounts/:id/password` | JWT | Self only |
| GET | `/health` | Public | All |

### Security Features
- **JWT access tokens** — 30-min HS256, claims include account ID, username, role
- **Rotating refresh tokens** — stored as SHA-256 hashes, reuse detection revokes all sessions
- **Password policy** — bcrypt cost 12, min 12 chars, 1 upper + 1 lower + 1 digit
- **Login lockout** — 15-minute lockout after 5 failed attempts (rolling window)
- **Anti-enumeration** — constant-time dummy bcrypt on invalid usernames
- **Device fingerprinting** — optional salted SHA-256 of user-agent + attributes
- **Rate limiting** — per-account (authenticated) or per-IP (public), with stale entry cleanup
- **Role guard middleware** — server-side role checks, no client trust
- **Sensitive field masking** — password_hash uses `json:"-"`, DTOs exclude it entirely
- **Session revocation** — on deactivation, freeze, or password change

### Deployment
- `docker-compose up` starts PostgreSQL 16 + API with auto-migration
- Single container, fully offline, no external dependencies

Scheduling & Match Lifecycle

  Build Match and Scheduling APIs in Go (Echo + sqlx + PostgreSQL).

  Season Setup: Support season creation and schedule generation/import
  with the following validations:
  - Home/away balance: No team may exceed 3 consecutive home or away
  games
  - Round integrity: No duplicate pairings within a round
  - Time/venue conflicts: A venue cannot host overlapping matches within
   90 minutes
  - Conflict resolution: Any override requires an explicit
  "override_reason" stored for audit

  Matches Table: teams, venue, scheduled_at, status, override_reason.

  Match Status Workflow: Draft → Scheduled → In-Progress → Final →
  Canceled. Referee/staff assignment is locked once In-Progress starts.
  Reassignment allowed only by Scheduler role with a documented reason.

  Assignments Table: referee/staff linked to matches with role,
  assigned_by, and reason for any reassignment.

  All state transitions and overrides logged to audit trail.

text.text:
Perfect! Now I have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary of the current state:

## Codebase Exploration Summary

### 1. Models (internal/models/)

**Account Model** (`account.go`)
- Defines Role enum: Administrator, Scheduler, Instructor, Reviewer, Finance Clerk, Auditor
- Defines Status enum: Active, Frozen, Deactivated
- Account struct with id, username, password_hash, role, status, created_at, updated_at
- Custom Scan/Value methods for database type conversions

**Device Model** (`device.go`)
- Device tracking with id, account_id, fingerprint_hash, device_name, last_seen_at, created_at
- Used for device-specific refresh tokens and login tracking

**LoginAttempt Model** (`login_attempt.go`)
- Tracks authentication attempts: id, account_id, success, ip_address, attempted_at
- Used for failed login tracking and brute-force protection

**RefreshToken Model** (`refresh_token.go`)
- Token storage: id, account_id, token_hash, device_id, expires_at, revoked, created_at
- Supports device-specific tokens and revocation

### 2. Router Setup (internal/router/router.go)

**Route Structure:**
- Public routes under `/auth` group with rate limiting:
  - `POST /auth/login` - Login endpoint
  - `POST /auth/refresh` - Token refresh endpoint

- Protected routes under `/api` group (JWT required + rate limiting):
  - `POST /api/auth/logout` - Logout (authenticated only)
  - `POST /api/accounts` - Create account (Administrator only)
  - `GET /api/accounts` - List accounts (Administrator only)
  - `PUT /api/accounts/:id/status` - Update account status (Administrator only)
  - `GET /api/accounts/:id` - Get account (authenticated, handler enforces self-access)
  - `PUT /api/accounts/:id/password` - Change password (authenticated, handler enforces self-only)

**Middleware Application:**
- Rate limiter applied to all routes
- JWT authentication on `/api` group
- Role-based access control (RequireRoles) on admin routes

### 3. Main Entry Point (cmd/server/main.go)

**Initialization Sequence:**
1. Load configuration from environment
2. Retry database connection (30 attempts, 2-second intervals)
3. Run migrations automatically
4. Initialize repositories (Account, RefreshToken, LoginAttempt, Device)
5. Initialize services (Token, Device, Auth, Account)
6. Initialize handlers (Auth, Account)
7. Initialize middleware (RateLimiter)
8. Setup Echo web framework with logging and recovery middleware
9. Configure health check endpoint (`GET /health`)
10. Setup routes via router.Setup()
11. Implement graceful shutdown (SIGINT/SIGTERM)

**Migration Loading:**
- Checks `MIGRATIONS_PATH` env var first
- Falls back to `./migrations` relative to working directory
- Falls back to migrations relative to executable path

### 4. Migrations (migrations/)

**Four Migration Files:**

1. **001_create_accounts.up.sql** - Creates accounts table with:
   - Custom ENUM types for roles and statuses
   - UUID primary key with default gen_random_uuid()
   - Unique constraint on username
   - Indexes on username and status

2. **002_create_refresh_tokens.up.sql** - Creates refresh_tokens table with:
   - Foreign key to accounts with CASCADE delete
   - Optional device_id field
   - Indexes on account_id and token_hash

3. **003_create_devices.up.sql** - Creates devices table with:
   - Foreign key to accounts with CASCADE delete
   - Unique constraint on (account_id, fingerprint_hash)
   - Index on account_id

4. **004_create_login_attempts.up.sql** - Creates login_attempts table with:
   - Tracks authentication attempts with IP addresses
   - Compound index on (account_id, attempted_at DESC)

All migrations have corresponding `.down.sql` files for rollback.

### 5. Config (internal/config/config.go)

**Configuration Parameters:**
- `ServerPort` - Server address (default: :8080)
- `DatabaseURL` - PostgreSQL connection string
- `JWTSecret` - JWT signing secret
- `AccessTokenTTL` - Access token expiration (default: 30 minutes)
- `RefreshTokenTTL` - Refresh token expiration (default: 7 days)
- `DeviceFingerprintSalt` - Salt for device fingerprinting
- `BcryptCost` - Password hashing cost (default: 12)
- `MaxLoginAttempts` - Failed login threshold (default: 5)
- `LockoutDuration` - Account lockout period (default: 15 minutes)
- `RateLimitRPS` - Rate limit requests per second (default: 10.0)
- `RateLimitBurst` - Rate limit burst capacity (default: 20)

**Config Loading Pattern:**
- Environment variable lookup with fallback defaults
- Helper functions: getEnv, getInt, getFloat, getDuration

### 6. Middleware Files

**jwt_auth.go**
- Validates Bearer token authorization header
- Extracts and validates JWT claims
- Sets `claims`, `account_id`, and `role` in Echo context
- Returns 401 for missing/invalid tokens

**role_guard.go**
- Middleware factory accepting variable number of allowed roles
- Checks role in Echo context against allowed set
- Returns 403 for insufficient permissions

**rate_limiter.go**
- Per-account or per-IP rate limiting using `golang.org/x/time/rate`
- Uses account_id for authenticated requests, IP for anonymous
- Cleanup goroutine removes stale limiters every 10 minutes
- Configurable RPS and burst capacity

### 7. Go Module (go.mod)

**Key Dependencies:**
- `github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5` - JWT token generation/validation
- `github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4` - Database migrations
- `github.com/google/uuid` - UUID generation
- `github.com/jmoiron/sqlx` - SQL database access with struct scanning
- `github.com/labstack/echo/v4` - Web framework
- `github.com/lib/pq` - PostgreSQL driver
- `golang.org/x/crypto` - Password hashing (bcrypt)
- `golang.org/x/time` - Rate limiting

**Go Version:** 1.22

---

## Key Architecture Patterns

1. **Dependency Injection** - Services are instantiated in main.go and passed to handlers
2. **Repository Pattern** - Database access is abstracted through repositories
3. **Service Layer** - Business logic separated from handlers
4. **Handler Pattern** - HTTP request handling decoupled from business logic
5. **Middleware Chain** - Composable middleware for authentication, authorization, rate limiting
6. **Environment Configuration** - All config driven by environment variables with sensible defaults
7. **Database-First** - Migrations run automatically on startup
8. **Graceful Shutdown** - Signal handling for clean server termination

{"file_path": "C:\Users\hp\Desktop\eaglepoint\task_106\repo\internal\router\router.go", "content": "package router

import (
	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/handler"
	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/middleware"
	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/models"
	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/service"
	"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
)

func Setup(
	e *echo.Echo,
	authHandler *handler.AuthHandler,
	accountHandler *handler.AccountHandler,
	seasonHandler *handler.SeasonHandler,
	matchHandler *handler.MatchHandler,
	tokenService *service.TokenService,
	rateLimiter *middleware.RateLimiter,
) {
	// Public routes (no JWT required)
	auth := e.Group("/auth")
	auth.Use(rateLimiter.Middleware())
	auth.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
	auth.POST("/refresh", authHandler.Refresh)

	// Protected routes (JWT required)
	api := e.Group("/api",
		middleware.JWTAuth(tokenService),
		rateLimiter.Middleware(),
	)

	// Logout requires authentication
	api.POST("/auth/logout", authHandler.Logout)

	// Account management - Administrator only
	accounts := api.Group("/accounts",
		middleware.RequireRoles(models.RoleAdministrator),
	)
	accounts.POST("", accountHandler.Create)
	accounts.GET("", accountHandler.List)
	accounts.PUT("/:id/status", accountHandler.UpdateStatus)

	// Get account - handler does self-access check internally
	api.GET("/accounts/:id", accountHandler.Get)

	// Change password - any authenticated user, handler enforces self-only
	api.PUT("/accounts/:id/password", accountHandler.ChangePassword)

	// Scheduling routes - Scheduler and Administrator
	schedulerRoles := middleware.RequireRoles(models.RoleScheduler, models.RoleAdministrator)

	// Seasons
	seasons := api.Group("/seasons")
	seasons.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateSeason, schedulerRoles)
	seasons.GET("", seasonHandler.ListSeasons)
	seasons.GET("/:id", seasonHandler.GetSeason)

	// Teams
	teams := api.Group("/teams")
	teams.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateTeam, schedulerRoles)
	teams.GET("/season/:season_id", seasonHandler.ListTeams)

	// Venues
	venues := api.Group("/venues")
	venues.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateVenue, schedulerRoles)
	venues.GET("", seasonHandler.ListVenues)

	// Matches
	matches := api.Group("/matches")
	matches.POST("", matchHandler.CreateMatch, schedulerRoles)
	matches.POST("/import", matchHandler.ImportSchedule, schedulerRoles)
	matches.GET("", matchHandler.ListMatches)
	matches.GET("/:id", matchHandler.GetMatch)
	matches.PUT("/:id", matchHandler.UpdateMatch, schedulerRoles)
	matches.PUT("/:id/status", matchHandler.TransitionStatus, schedulerRoles)

	// Match Assignments
	assignments := api.Group("/assignments")
	assignments.POST("", matchHandler.CreateAssignment, schedulerRoles)
	assignments.GET("/match/:match_id", matchHandler.ListAssignments)
	assignments.PUT("/:id/reassign", matchHandler.ReassignAssignment, schedulerRoles)
	assignments.DELETE("/:id", matchHandler.DeleteAssignment, schedulerRoles)
}
"}

func Setup(
	e *echo.Echo,
	authHandler *handler.AuthHandler,
	accountHandler *handler.AccountHandler,
	seasonHandler *handler.SeasonHandler,
	matchHandler *handler.MatchHandler,
	tokenService *service.TokenService,
	rateLimiter *middleware.RateLimiter,
) {
	// Public routes (no JWT required)
	auth := e.Group("/auth")
	auth.Use(rateLimiter.Middleware())
	auth.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
	auth.POST("/refresh", authHandler.Refresh)

	// Protected routes (JWT required)
	api := e.Group("/api",
		middleware.JWTAuth(tokenService),
		rateLimiter.Middleware(),
	)

	// Logout requires authentication
	api.POST("/auth/logout", authHandler.Logout)

	// Account management - Administrator only
	accounts := api.Group("/accounts",
		middleware.RequireRoles(models.RoleAdministrator),
	)
	accounts.POST("", accountHandler.Create)
	accounts.GET("", accountHandler.List)
	accounts.PUT("/:id/status", accountHandler.UpdateStatus)

	// Get account - handler does self-access check internally
	api.GET("/accounts/:id", accountHandler.Get)

	// Change password - any authenticated user, handler enforces self-only
	api.PUT("/accounts/:id/password", accountHandler.ChangePassword)

	// Scheduling routes - Scheduler and Administrator
	schedulerRoles := middleware.RequireRoles(models.RoleScheduler, models.RoleAdministrator)

	// Seasons
	seasons := api.Group("/seasons")
	seasons.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateSeason, schedulerRoles)
	seasons.GET("", seasonHandler.ListSeasons)
	seasons.GET("/:id", seasonHandler.GetSeason)

	// Teams
	teams := api.Group("/teams")
	teams.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateTeam, schedulerRoles)
	teams.GET("/season/:season_id", seasonHandler.ListTeams)

	// Venues
	venues := api.Group("/venues")
	venues.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateVenue, schedulerRoles)
	venues.GET("", seasonHandler.ListVenues)

	// Matches
	matches := api.Group("/matches")
	matches.POST("", matchHandler.CreateMatch, schedulerRoles)
	matches.POST("/import", matchHandler.ImportSchedule, schedulerRoles)
	matches.GET("", matchHandler.ListMatches)
	matches.GET("/:id", matchHandler.GetMatch)
	matches.PUT("/:id", matchHandler.UpdateMatch, schedulerRoles)
	matches.PUT("/:id/status", matchHandler.TransitionStatus, schedulerRoles)

	// Match Assignments
	assignments := api.Group("/assignments")
	assignments.POST("", matchHandler.CreateAssignment, schedulerRoles)
	assignments.GET("/match/:match_id", matchHandler.ListAssignments)
	assignments.PUT("/:id/reassign", matchHandler.ReassignAssignment, schedulerRoles)
	assignments.DELETE("/:id", matchHandler.DeleteAssignment, schedulerRoles)
}
"}

3	import (

15		"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/middleware"

18		"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/service"
19		"github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4"
20		"github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4/database/postgres"
21		_ "github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4/source/file"
22		"github.com/jmoiron/sqlx"
23		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
24		echomw "github.com/labstack/echo/v4/middleware"
25		_ "github.com/lib/pq"
26	)

28	func main() {
29		cfg := config.Load()
30	
31		// Connect to database with retry
32		var db *sqlx.DB
33		var err error
34		for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
35			db, err = sqlx.Connect("postgres", cfg.DatabaseURL)
36			if err == nil {
37				break
38			}
39			log.Printf("waiting for database... attempt %d/30: %v", i+1, err)
40			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
41		}
42		if err != nil {
43			log.Fatalf("failed to connect to database: %v", err)
44		}
45		defer db.Close()
46	
47		log.Println("connected to database")
48	
49		// Run migrations
50		if err := runMigrations(db); err != nil {
51			log.Fatalf("failed to run migrations: %v", err)
52		}
53		log.Println("migrations complete")
54	
55		// Initialize repositories
56		accountRepo := repository.NewAccountRepository(db)
57		refreshTokenRepo := repository.NewRefreshTokenRepository(db)
58		loginAttemptRepo := repository.NewLoginAttemptRepository(db)
59		deviceRepo := repository.NewDeviceRepository(db)
60	
61		// Initialize services
62		tokenService := service.NewTokenService(cfg)
63		deviceService := service.NewDeviceService(deviceRepo, cfg)
64		authService := service.NewAuthService(accountRepo, refreshTokenRepo, loginAttemptRepo, tokenService, deviceService, cfg)
65		accountService := service.NewAccountService(accountRepo, refreshTokenRepo, cfg)
66	
67		// Initialize handlers
68		authHandler := handler.NewAuthHandler(authService)
69		accountHandler := handler.NewAccountHandler(accountService)
70	
71		// Initialize middleware
72		rateLimiter := middleware.NewRateLimiter(cfg.RateLimitRPS, cfg.RateLimitBurst)
73	
74		// Setup Echo
75		e := echo.New()
76		e.HideBanner = true
77		e.Use(echomw.Logger())
78		e.Use(echomw.Recover())
79	
80		// Health check
81		e.GET("/health", func(c echo.Context) error {
82			return c.JSON(200, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
83		})
84	
85		// Setup routes
86		router.Setup(e, authHandler, accountHandler, tokenService, rateLimiter)
87	
88		// Graceful shutdown
89		go func() {
90			if err := e.Start(cfg.ServerPort); err != nil {
91				log.Printf("server stopped: %v", err)
92			}
93		}()
94	
95		quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
96		signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
97		<-quit
98	
99		log.Println("shutting down server...")
100		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
101		defer cancel()
102		if err := e.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
103			log.Fatalf("server forced shutdown: %v", err)
104		}
105		log.Println("server stopped")
106	}
107	
108	func runMigrations(db *sqlx.DB) error {
109		driver, err := postgres.WithInstance(db.DB, &postgres.Config{})
110		if err != nil {
111			return fmt.Errorf("creating migration driver: %w", err)
112		}
113	
114		// Determine migrations path
115		migrationsPath := getMigrationsPath()
116		m, err := migrate.NewWithDatabaseInstance(
117			"file://"+migrationsPath,
118			"postgres",
119			driver,
120		)
121		if err != nil {
122			return fmt.Errorf("creating migrate instance: %w", err)
123		}
124	
125		if err := m.Up(); err != nil && err != migrate.ErrNoChange {
126			return fmt.Errorf("running migrations: %w", err)
127		}
128		return nil
129	}
130	
131	func getMigrationsPath() string {
132		// Check for MIGRATIONS_PATH env var first
133		if p := os.Getenv("MIGRATIONS_PATH"); p != "" {
134			return p
135		}
136		// Try relative to working directory
137		if _, err := os.Stat("migrations"); err == nil {
138			return "migrations"
139		}
140		// Try relative to executable
141		ex, _ := os.Executable()
142		return filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(ex), "migrations")
143	}

All builds clean. Here's what was added:

## New Files (26 files)

### Database Migrations (005-010)
- **`seasons`** — name, start/end dates, status enum (Planning/Active/Completed/Archived)
- **`teams`** — name unique per season, FK to seasons
- **`venues`** — name, location, capacity
- **`matches`** — round, home/away teams, venue, scheduled_at, status enum, override_reason, CHECK constraint preventing same-team matchups
- **`match_assignments`** — referee/staff linked to matches with role, assigned_by, reassignment_reason, UNIQUE per (match, account, role)
- **`audit_logs`** — entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_id, JSONB details

### Match Status Workflow
**Draft → Scheduled → In-Progress → Final → Canceled** — enforced by `ValidMatchTransitions` map in `models/match.go`

### Scheduling Validations (in `match_service.go`)
| Rule | Implementation |
|------|---------------|
| Home/away balance | Checks last 3 games — rejects if team would exceed 3 consecutive home or away |
| Round integrity | SQL query detects duplicate pairings (either direction) within a round |
| Venue conflicts | SQL query checks for matches within 90 minutes at the same venue |
| Override mechanism | Any violation requires `override_reason` — stored on match + logged to audit |

### Assignment Locking
- Assignments can be created/modified only when match is Draft or Scheduled
- Once In-Progress/Final/Canceled, all assignment mutations return `ErrAssignmentLocked`
- Reassignment requires a documented `reason` field, logged to audit trail

### New API Endpoints
| Method | Path | Access |
|--------|------|--------|
| POST | `/api/seasons` | Scheduler, Administrator |
| GET | `/api/seasons` | Any authenticated |
| GET | `/api/seasons/:id` | Any authenticated |
| POST | `/api/teams` | Scheduler, Administrator |
| GET | `/api/teams/season/:season_id` | Any authenticated |
| POST | `/api/venues` | Scheduler, Administrator |
| GET | `/api/venues` | Any authenticated |
| POST | `/api/matches` | Scheduler, Administrator |
| POST | `/api/matches/import` | Scheduler, Administrator |
| GET | `/api/matches?season_id=&round=` | Any authenticated |
| GET | `/api/matches/:id` | Any authenticated |
| PUT | `/api/matches/:id` | Scheduler, Administrator (Draft only) |
| PUT | `/api/matches/:id/status` | Scheduler, Administrator |
| POST | `/api/assignments` | Scheduler, Administrator |
| GET | `/api/assignments/match/:match_id` | Any authenticated |
| PUT | `/api/assignments/:id/reassign` | Scheduler, Administrator |
| DELETE | `/api/assignments/:id` | Scheduler, Administrator |

### Audit Trail
Every state transition, match creation/update, assignment creation/reassignment/deletion is logged to `audit_logs` with actor ID, action, and JSONB details (including any overridden violations and reasons).

Courses & Teaching Resources

  Build Course and Resource APIs in Go (Echo + sqlx + PostgreSQL).

  Course Management: Model course metadata and a structured chapter/unit
   outline. Course outline stored as a tree using course_outline_nodes
  table with parent_id and order_index.

  Resource Management: Each resource is a document, video file, or link
  (plain text). Support immutable versioning with a latest pointer.
  resources table links to resource_versions table containing: sha256
  file fingerprint, size in bytes, mime type (validated against
  allowlist), and storage_path on local disk.

  Tagging: Max 20 tags per resource, each tag max 32 characters.

  Full-Text Search: Search over titles, descriptions, and extracted text
   for PDF and DOCX files only.

  Visibility: Private to course staff vs. enrolled users. Preview and
  download permissions enforced by role and course membership.

  File Storage: Local disk, SHA-256 fingerprints for deduplication and
  integrity.

text.text:
Perfect! I've read all the specified files. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document of the patterns and structure you'll need to follow when adding new Course and Resource modules.

## Codebase Architecture & Pattern Analysis

### 1. **Routing Pattern (internal/router/router.go)**

**Key Structure:**
- Routes are organized into logical groups using Echo's `Group()` method
- Public routes and protected routes are clearly separated
- Role-based access control is applied via middleware at the group level
- Each resource type gets its own group with consistent middleware application

**Pattern Example:**
```
api := e.Group("/api", middleware.JWTAuth(tokenService), rateLimiter.Middleware())

// Role-restricted group
schedulerRoles := middleware.RequireRoles(models.RoleScheduler, models.RoleAdministrator)

seasons := api.Group("/seasons")
seasons.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateSeason, schedulerRoles)
seasons.GET("", seasonHandler.ListSeasons)
seasons.GET("/:id", seasonHandler.GetSeason)
```

**What You Need:**
- Group definition with path prefix
- Middleware applied at group level for authorization
- HTTP methods mapped to handler functions
- Role guards applied to write operations (POST, PUT, DELETE)

---

### 2. **Dependency Injection / Initialization (cmd/server/main.go)**

**Wiring Pattern:**
```
1. Initialize Repositories: 
   repo := repository.NewXxxRepository(db)

2. Initialize Services: 
   service := service.NewXxxService(repo, otherDeps, config)

3. Initialize Handlers: 
   handler := handler.NewXxxHandler(service)

4. Register in Router: 
   router.Setup(e, handler1, handler2, ..., tokenService, rateLimiter)
```

**For Course & Resource modules, you'll need:**
- `repository.NewCourseRepository(db)`
- `repository.NewResourceRepository(db)`
- `service.NewCourseService(courseRepo, auditService)`
- `service.NewResourceService(resourceRepo, courseRepo, auditService)`
- `handler.NewCourseHandler(courseService)`
- `handler.NewResourceHandler(resourceService)`
- Add to `router.Setup()` function signature and call

---

### 3. **Service Layer Pattern (internal/service/audit_service.go)**

**Service Structure:**
- Constructor: `NewXxxService(dependencies) *XxxService`
- Struct embeds repository: `repo *repository.XxxRepository`
- Methods take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter
- Business logic is here, not in handlers or repos

**Audit Service Example:**
```go
type AuditService struct {
    repo *repository.AuditLogRepository
}

func NewAuditService(repo *repository.AuditLogRepository) *AuditService {
    return &AuditService{repo: repo}
}

func (s *AuditService) Log(ctx context.Context, entityType string, entityID, actorID uuid.UUID, action string, details interface{}) {
    // Validation and data transformation
    entry := &models.AuditLog{...}
    s.repo.Create(ctx, entry)
}
```

**Key Points:**
- Services hold repositories
- Services call audit logging for important operations
- Input validation happens in services
- Pagination limits are enforced (20 default, 100 max)

---

### 4. **Repository Layer Pattern (internal/repository/audit_log_repo.go)**

**Repository Structure:**
- Constructor: `NewXxxRepository(db *sqlx.DB) *XxxRepository`
- Methods use context: `func (r *XxxRepository) MethodName(ctx context.Context, ...) (..., error)`
- SQL queries with parameterized placeholders ($1, $2, etc.)
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("audit_log_repo.MethodName: %w", err)`

**Standard Methods Pattern:**
- `Create(ctx, entity) error`
- `GetByID(ctx, id) (*Entity, error)`
- `ListByEntity(ctx, entityType, entityID, offset, limit) ([]Entity, error)`
- `Update(ctx, entity) error`
- `Delete(ctx, id) error`

**Audit Log Example:**
```go
type AuditLogRepository struct {
    db *sqlx.DB
}

func (r *AuditLogRepository) Create(ctx context.Context, log *models.AuditLog) error {
    query := `INSERT INTO audit_logs (...) VALUES ($1, $2, ...)`
    _, err := r.db.ExecContext(ctx, query, ...)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("audit_log_repo.Create: %w", err)
    }
    return nil
}
```

---

### 5. **Middleware Pattern**

**JWT Auth Middleware (internal/middleware/jwt_auth.go):**
```go
func JWTAuth(tokenService *service.TokenService) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
    return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
        return func(c echo.Context) error {
            // Extract token from Authorization header
            // Validate token
            // Set context values: "claims", "account_id", "role"
            return next(c)
        }
    }
}
```

**Role Guard Middleware (internal/middleware/role_guard.go):**
```go
func RequireRoles(roles ...models.Role) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
    allowed := make(map[models.Role]bool, len(roles))
    for _, r := range roles {
        allowed[r] = true
    }
    return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
        return func(c echo.Context) error {
            role, ok := c.Get("role").(models.Role)
            if !ok || !allowed[role] {
                return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusForbidden, "insufficient permissions")
            }
            return next(c)
        }
    }
}
```

**Context Values Set by Middleware:**
- `claims` (TokenClaims)
- `account_id` (uuid.UUID)
- `role` (models.Role)

---

### 6. **Models/Entity Structure (internal/models/account.go)**

**Pattern:**
- Use `uuid.UUID` for IDs with `db:"id" json:"id"` tags
- Use `time.Time` for timestamps with `db:"created_at" json:"created_at"` tags
- Database field names use snake_case in `db:""` tags
- JSON field names match Go field names (camelCase by default)
- Sensitive fields marked with `json:"-"` to exclude from serialization

**Role Constants in Models:**
```go
type Role string

const (
    RoleAdministrator Role = "Administrator"
    RoleScheduler     Role = "Scheduler"
    RoleInstructor    Role = "Instructor"
    RoleReviewer      Role = "Reviewer"
    RoleFinanceClerk  Role = "Finance Clerk"
    RoleAuditor       Role = "Auditor"
)

var ValidRoles = map[Role]bool{...}
```

**Custom Type Methods:**
- Implement `Scan(value interface{}) error` for database deserialization
- Implement `Value() (driver.Value, error)` for database serialization
- Handle both string and []byte conversions

---

### 7. **Configuration Pattern (internal/config/config.go)**

**Config Struct Pattern:**
- All config fields exported (capitalized)
- Load from environment variables with fallback defaults
- Environment variable naming: `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE`
- Helper functions: `getEnv()`, `getInt()`, `getFloat()`, `getDuration()`

---

### 8. **Audit Integration Pattern**

**How Audit Logging Works:**
1. Service receives audit service in constructor
2. After successful operations, call: `s.auditService.Log(ctx, entityType, entityID, actorID, action, details)`
3. Audit service does best-effort logging (doesn't fail main operation)
4. Entity type strings: "Season", "Team", "Venue", "Match", "Assignment"
5. Action strings: "created", "updated", "deleted", "transitioned"
6. Details: can be any struct, gets JSON marshaled

**Example Integration:**
```go
type SeasonService struct {
    seasonRepo   *repository.SeasonRepository
    auditService *service.AuditService
}

func (s *SeasonService) CreateSeason(ctx context.Context, season *models.Season, actorID uuid.UUID) (*models.Season, error) {
    // Validation and creation logic
    if err := s.seasonRepo.Create(ctx, season); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    // Log the action
    s.auditService.Log(ctx, "Season", season.ID, actorID, "created", map[string]interface{}{
        "name": season.Name,
    })
    return season, nil
}
```

---

## Summary: Adding Course & Resource Modules

You'll need these files for each new module:

1. **Models** (`internal/models/course.go`, `internal/models/resource.go`)
   - Define structs with UUID IDs, timestamps
   - Use json and db tags correctly

2. **Repositories** (`internal/repository/course_repo.go`, `internal/repository/resource_repo.go`)
   - Constructor accepting `*sqlx.DB`
   - Methods: Create, GetByID, List, Update, Delete
   - All methods use context and error wrapping

3. **Services** (`internal/service/course_service.go`, `internal/service/resource_service.go`)
   - Constructor accepting repos and `*AuditService`
   - Business logic methods with context
   - Call audit logging after operations

4. **Handlers** (`internal/handler/course_handler.go`, `internal/handler/resource_handler.go`)
   - Constructor accepting service
   - HTTP handler methods: Create, Get, List, Update, Delete
   - Extract actorID from context: `c.Get("account_id").(uuid.UUID)`

5. **Router Setup** (update `internal/router/router.go`)
   - Add handler parameters to `Setup()` function
   - Create route groups with appropriate middleware
   - Apply role guards to write operations

6. **Main.go** (update `cmd/server/main.go`)
   - Initialize repositories
   - Initialize services with audit service dependency
   - Initialize handlers
   - Pass to router.Setup()

7. **Migrations** (`migrations/`)
   - Create `up` and `down` migration files for database schema

All files are located at:
- `/Users/hp/Desktop/eaglepoint/task_106/repo/internal/router/router.go`
- `/Users/hp/Desktop/eaglepoint/task_106/repo/cmd/server/main.go`
- `/Users/hp/Desktop/eaglepoint/task_106/repo/internal/config/config.go`
- `/Users/hp/Desktop/eaglepoint/task_106/repo/internal/models/account.go`
- `/Users/hp/Desktop/eaglepoint/task_106/repo/internal/service/audit_service.go`
- `/Users/hp/Desktop/eaglepoint/task_106/repo/internal/repository/audit_log_repo.go`
- `/Users/hp/Desktop/eaglepoint/task_106/repo/internal/middleware/role_guard.go`

{"file_path": "C:\Users\hp\Desktop\eaglepoint\task_106\repo\internal\dto\resource_dto.go", "content": "package dto

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/models"
	"github.com/google/uuid"
)

type CreateResourceRequest struct {
	CourseID     string   `json:"course_id"`
	NodeID       *string  `json:"node_id,omitempty"`
	Title        string   `json:"title"`
	Description  *string  `json:"description,omitempty"`
	ResourceType string   `json:"resource_type"`
	Visibility   string   `json:"visibility"`
	LinkURL      *string  `json:"link_url,omitempty"`
	Tags         []string `json:"tags,omitempty"`
}

type UpdateResourceRequest struct {
	Title       *string  `json:"title,omitempty"`
	Description *string  `json:"description,omitempty"`
	Visibility  *string  `json:"visibility,omitempty"`
	NodeID      *string  `json:"node_id,omitempty"`
	LinkURL     *string  `json:"link_url,omitempty"`
	Tags        []string `json:"tags,omitempty"`
}

type ResourceResponse struct {
	ID              uuid.UUID  `json:"id"`
	CourseID        uuid.UUID  `json:"course_id"`
	NodeID          *uuid.UUID `json:"node_id,omitempty"`
	Title           string     `json:"title"`
	Description     *string    `json:"description,omitempty"`
	ResourceType    string     `json:"resource_type"`
	Visibility      string     `json:"visibility"`
	LinkURL         *string    `json:"link_url,omitempty"`
	LatestVersionID *uuid.UUID `json:"latest_version_id,omitempty"`
	Tags            []string   `json:"tags"`
	CreatedBy       uuid.UUID  `json:"created_by"`
	CreatedAt       time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
	UpdatedAt       time.Time  `json:"updated_at"`
}

func ToResourceResponse(r *models.Resource, tags []string) ResourceResponse {
	if tags == nil {
		tags = []string{}
	}
	return ResourceResponse{
		ID:              r.ID,
		CourseID:        r.CourseID,
		NodeID:          r.NodeID,
		Title:           r.Title,
		Description:     r.Description,
		ResourceType:    string(r.ResourceType),
		Visibility:      string(r.Visibility),
		LinkURL:         r.LinkURL,
		LatestVersionID: r.LatestVersionID,
		Tags:            tags,
		CreatedBy:       r.CreatedBy,
		CreatedAt:       r.CreatedAt,
		UpdatedAt:       r.UpdatedAt,
	}
}

type VersionResponse struct {
	ID            uuid.UUID `json:"id"`
	ResourceID    uuid.UUID `json:"resource_id"`
	VersionNumber int       `json:"version_number"`
	FileName      string    `json:"file_name"`
	MimeType      string    `json:"mime_type"`
	SizeBytes     int64     `json:"size_bytes"`
	SHA256Hash    string    `json:"sha256_hash"`
	UploadedBy    uuid.UUID `json:"uploaded_by"`
	CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at"`
}

func ToVersionResponse(v *models.ResourceVersion) VersionResponse {
	return VersionResponse{
		ID:            v.ID,
		ResourceID:    v.ResourceID,
		VersionNumber: v.VersionNumber,
		FileName:      v.FileName,
		MimeType:      v.MimeType,
		SizeBytes:     v.SizeBytes,
		SHA256Hash:    v.SHA256Hash,
		UploadedBy:    v.UploadedBy,
		CreatedAt:     v.CreatedAt,
	}
}

func ToVersionResponseList(versions []models.ResourceVersion) []VersionResponse {
	result := make([]VersionResponse, len(versions))
	for i, v := range versions {
		result[i] = ToVersionResponse(&v)
	}
	return result
}
"}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\hp\Desktop\eaglepoint\task_106\repo\internal\router\router.go", "content": "package router

import (
	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/handler"
	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/middleware"
	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/models"
	"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/service"
	"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
)

func Setup(
	e *echo.Echo,
	authHandler *handler.AuthHandler,
	accountHandler *handler.AccountHandler,
	seasonHandler *handler.SeasonHandler,
	matchHandler *handler.MatchHandler,
	courseHandler *handler.CourseHandler,
	resourceHandler *handler.ResourceHandler,
	tokenService *service.TokenService,
	rateLimiter *middleware.RateLimiter,
) {
	// Public routes (no JWT required)
	auth := e.Group("/auth")
	auth.Use(rateLimiter.Middleware())
	auth.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
	auth.POST("/refresh", authHandler.Refresh)

	// Protected routes (JWT required)
	api := e.Group("/api",
		middleware.JWTAuth(tokenService),
		rateLimiter.Middleware(),
	)

	// Logout requires authentication
	api.POST("/auth/logout", authHandler.Logout)

	// Account management - Administrator only
	accounts := api.Group("/accounts",
		middleware.RequireRoles(models.RoleAdministrator),
	)
	accounts.POST("", accountHandler.Create)
	accounts.GET("", accountHandler.List)
	accounts.PUT("/:id/status", accountHandler.UpdateStatus)

	// Get account - handler does self-access check internally
	api.GET("/accounts/:id", accountHandler.Get)

	// Change password - any authenticated user, handler enforces self-only
	api.PUT("/accounts/:id/password", accountHandler.ChangePassword)

	// Scheduling routes - Scheduler and Administrator
	schedulerRoles := middleware.RequireRoles(models.RoleScheduler, models.RoleAdministrator)

	// Seasons
	seasons := api.Group("/seasons")
	seasons.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateSeason, schedulerRoles)
	seasons.GET("", seasonHandler.ListSeasons)
	seasons.GET("/:id", seasonHandler.GetSeason)

	// Teams
	teams := api.Group("/teams")
	teams.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateTeam, schedulerRoles)
	teams.GET("/season/:season_id", seasonHandler.ListTeams)

	// Venues
	venues := api.Group("/venues")
	venues.POST("", seasonHandler.CreateVenue, schedulerRoles)
	venues.GET("", seasonHandler.ListVenues)

	// Matches
	matches := api.Group("/matches")
	matches.POST("", matchHandler.CreateMatch, schedulerRoles)
	matches.POST("/import", matchHandler.ImportSchedule, schedulerRoles)
	matches.GET("", matchHandler.ListMatches)
	matches.GET("/:id", matchHandler.GetMatch)
	matches.PUT("/:id", matchHandler.UpdateMatch, schedulerRoles)
	matches.PUT("/:id/status", matchHandler.TransitionStatus, schedulerRoles)

	// Match Assignments
	assignments := api.Group("/assignments")
	assignments.POST("", matchHandler.CreateAssignment, schedulerRoles)
	assignments.GET("/match/:match_id", matchHandler.ListAssignments)
	assignments.PUT("/:id/reassign", matchHandler.ReassignAssignment, schedulerRoles)
	assignments.DELETE("/:id", matchHandler.DeleteAssignment, schedulerRoles)

	// Course routes - Instructor and Administrator can create/manage
	instructorRoles := middleware.RequireRoles(models.RoleInstructor, models.RoleAdministrator)

	courses := api.Group("/courses")
	courses.POST("", courseHandler.CreateCourse, instructorRoles)
	courses.GET("", courseHandler.ListCourses)
	courses.GET("/:id", courseHandler.GetCourse)
	courses.PUT("/:id", courseHandler.UpdateCourse, instructorRoles)

	// Course outline (tree structure)
	outline := api.Group("/outline-nodes")
	outline.POST("", courseHandler.CreateOutlineNode, instructorRoles)
	outline.GET("/course/:course_id", courseHandler.GetOutlineTree)
	outline.PUT("/:id", courseHandler.UpdateOutlineNode, instructorRoles)
	outline.DELETE("/:id", courseHandler.DeleteOutlineNode, instructorRoles)

	// Course memberships
	members := api.Group("/courses/:course_id/members")
	members.POST("", courseHandler.AddMember, instructorRoles)
	members.GET("", courseHandler.ListMembers)
	members.DELETE("/:id", courseHandler.RemoveMember, instructorRoles)

	// Resources
	resources := api.Group("/resources")
	resources.POST("", resourceHandler.CreateResource, instructorRoles)
	resources.GET("", resourceHandler.ListResources)
	resources.GET("/search", resourceHandler.SearchResources)
	resources.GET("/:id", resourceHandler.GetResource)
	resources.PUT("/:id", resourceHandler.UpdateResource, instructorRoles)

	// Resource versions (file upload/download)
	resources.POST("/:id/versions", resourceHandler.UploadVersion, instructorRoles)
	resources.GET("/:id/versions", resourceHandler.ListVersions)
	resources.GET("/versions/:version_id/download", resourceHandler.DownloadVersion)
	resources.GET("/versions/:version_id/preview", resourceHandler.PreviewVersion)
}
"}

	// Course memberships
	members := api.Group("/courses/:course_id/members")

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"log"
7		"os"
8		"os/signal"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"syscall"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/config"
14		"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/handler"
15		"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/middleware"
16		"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/repository"
17		"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/router"
18		"github.com/eaglepoint/authapi/internal/service"
19		"github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4"
20		"github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4/database/postgres"
21		_ "github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4/source/file"
22		"github.com/jmoiron/sqlx"
23		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
24		echomw "github.com/labstack/echo/v4/middleware"
25		_ "github.com/lib/pq"
26	)
27	
28	func main() {
29		cfg := config.Load()
30	
31		// Connect to database with retry
32		var db *sqlx.DB
33		var err error
34		for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
35			db, err = sqlx.Connect("postgres", cfg.DatabaseURL)
36			if err == nil {
37				break
38			}
39			log.Printf("waiting for database... attempt %d/30: %v", i+1, err)
40			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
41		}
42		if err != nil {
43			log.Fatalf("failed to connect to database: %v", err)
44		}
45		defer db.Close()
46	
47		log.Println("connected to database")
48	
49		// Run migrations
50		if err := runMigrations(db); err != nil {
51			log.Fatalf("failed to run migrations: %v", err)
52		}
53		log.Println("migrations complete")
54	
55		// Initialize repositories
56		accountRepo := repository.NewAccountRepository(db)
57		refreshTokenRepo := repository.NewRefreshTokenRepository(db)
58		loginAttemptRepo := repository.NewLoginAttemptRepository(db)
59		deviceRepo := repository.NewDeviceRepository(db)
60		seasonRepo := repository.NewSeasonRepository(db)
61		teamRepo := repository.NewTeamRepository(db)
62		venueRepo := repository.NewVenueRepository(db)
63		matchRepo := repository.NewMatchRepository(db)
64		assignmentRepo := repository.NewMatchAssignmentRepository(db)
65		auditLogRepo := repository.NewAuditLogRepository(db)
66	
67		// Initialize services
68		tokenService := service.NewTokenService(cfg)
69		deviceService := service.NewDeviceService(deviceRepo, cfg)
70		authService := service.NewAuthService(accountRepo, refreshTokenRepo, loginAttemptRepo, tokenService, deviceService, cfg)
71		accountService := service.NewAccountService(accountRepo, refreshTokenRepo, cfg)
72		auditService := service.NewAuditService(auditLogRepo)
73		seasonService := service.NewSeasonService(seasonRepo, teamRepo, venueRepo, auditService)
74		matchService := service.NewMatchService(matchRepo, seasonRepo, teamRepo, venueRepo, assignmentRepo, auditService)
75	
76		// Initialize handlers
77		authHandler := handler.NewAuthHandler(authService)
78		accountHandler := handler.NewAccountHandler(accountService)
79		seasonHandler := handler.NewSeasonHandler(seasonService)
80		matchHandler := handler.NewMatchHandler(matchService)
81	
82		// Initialize middleware
83		rateLimiter := middleware.NewRateLimiter(cfg.RateLimitRPS, cfg.RateLimitBurst)
84	
85		// Setup Echo
86		e := echo.New()
87		e.HideBanner = true
88		e.Use(echomw.Logger())
89		e.Use(echomw.Recover())
90	
91		// Health check
92		e.GET("/health", func(c echo.Context) error {
93			return c.JSON(200, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
94		})
95	
96		// Setup routes
97		router.Setup(e, authHandler, accountHandler, seasonHandler, matchHandler, tokenService, rateLimiter)
98	
99		// Graceful shutdown
100		go func() {

All 93 source files compile cleanly. Here's a summary of what was added for the Courses & Teaching Resources module:

## New Database Tables (migrations 011-017)

| Table | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `courses` | Course metadata with status enum (Draft/Published/Archived) |
| `course_outline_nodes` | Tree structure via `parent_id` + `order_index`, types: Chapter/Unit |
| `course_memberships` | Staff vs Enrolled role per account per course (unique constraint) |
| `resources` | Documents/Videos/Links tied to courses and optionally outline nodes |
| `resource_versions` | Immutable versions with SHA-256 hash, size, mime type, storage path |
| `resource_tags` | Max 20 tags per resource, 32 char limit, unique per resource |
| Full-text search | GIN-indexed `tsvector` columns on resources (title/description weighted A/B) and resource_versions (extracted text for PDF/DOCX) |

## New API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Access |
|--------|------|--------|
| POST | `/api/courses` | Instructor, Administrator |
| GET | `/api/courses` | Any auth (admins/instructors see all; others see Published) |
| GET | `/api/courses/:id` | Any authenticated |
| PUT | `/api/courses/:id` | Instructor, Administrator (course staff only) |
| POST | `/api/outline-nodes` | Instructor, Administrator (course staff) |
| GET | `/api/outline-nodes/course/:course_id` | Any auth (returns tree) |
| PUT | `/api/outline-nodes/:id` | Instructor, Administrator (course staff) |
| DELETE | `/api/outline-nodes/:id` | Instructor, Administrator (course staff) |
| POST | `/api/courses/:course_id/members` | Instructor, Administrator (course staff) |
| GET | `/api/courses/:course_id/members` | Any authenticated |
| DELETE | `/api/courses/:course_id/members/:id` | Instructor, Administrator |
| POST | `/api/resources` | Instructor, Administrator |
| GET | `/api/resources?course_id=` | Any auth (visibility-filtered) |
| GET | `/api/resources/search?course_id=&q=` | Any auth (full-text search) |
| GET | `/api/resources/:id` | Access-controlled by visibility |
| PUT | `/api/resources/:id` | Instructor, Administrator |
| POST | `/api/resources/:id/versions` | Instructor, Administrator (multipart upload) |
| GET | `/api/resources/:id/versions` | Any authenticated |
| GET | `/api/resources/versions/:version_id/download` | Access-controlled + SHA-256 integrity check |
| GET | `/api/resources/versions/:version_id/preview` | Access-controlled (inline display) |

## Key Features

- **Course outline tree** — `parent_id` self-referential FK with `order_index` ordering, returned as nested JSON tree via `BuildOutlineTree()`
- **Immutable versioning** — each upload creates a new `resource_version`, `latest_version_id` pointer updated atomically; versions are never modified
- **SHA-256 deduplication** — on upload, content is hashed; if same hash exists, file storage is reused
- **Integrity verification** — downloads re-hash the file and compare against stored hash before serving
- **MIME allowlist** — 20+ allowed types across documents, videos, and images; validated on upload
- **Full-text search** — PostgreSQL `tsvector`/`plainto_tsquery` across resource titles (weight A), descriptions (weight B), and extracted text from PDF/DOCX
- **Visibility enforcement** — Staff-only resources hidden from Enrolled users; Administrators bypass all checks
- **Tag constraints** — max 20 tags, max 32 chars each; atomic replace via transaction
- **Auto-membership** — course creator automatically added as Staff member
- **Local disk storage** — files stored at `{STORAGE_PATH}/{course_id}/{resource_id}/{version_id}`, Docker volume mounted

Content Moderation & Risk Control

  Build Moderation APIs in Go (Echo + sqlx + PostgreSQL).

  Sensitive-Word Checks: Use locally managed dictionaries. Return match
  highlighting showing which words triggered the check. Dictionaries are
   CRUD-managed by Administrator.

  Manual Review States: Pending → Approved → Rejected. All moderation
  decisions logged with moderator, timestamp, and reason.

  Report Handling: Reports include category, evidence attachments (local
   files), and progress notes. Reports table tracks reporter, target
  content, category, status, and resolution.

  Anti-Spam Limits:
  - 60 write operations/minute per account
  - 10 reports/day per reporter

  Device Fingerprinting: Optional, using salted local hashes of user
  agent + stable device attributes. Fingerprint_hash is unique per
  account in the devices table.

  All moderation actions written to audit log.
